c-r2-sco-mor.note: Scotland and Morocco have met only once, at the 1998 World Cup (Morocco won 3-0) — also their only all-time meeting.

i-r1-fra-sen.note: France and Senegal have met only once, at the 2002 World Cup opening match (Senegal won 1-0) — also their only all-time meeting.

j-r3-alg-aut.note: Algeria and Austria have met only once, at the 1982 World Cup (Austria won 2-0) — also their only all-time meeting.

l-r3-pan-eng.note: Panama and England have met only once, at the 2018 World Cup (England won 6-1) — also their only all-time meeting.

e-r3-ecu-ger.source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2006_FIFA_World_Cup_Group_A (Germany 3-0, 20 Jun 2006); ESPN match report for the 2013 friendly, Germany 4-2 (29 May 2013): https://www.espn.com/soccer/match/_/gameId/361584/alemania-ecuador
e-r3-ecu-ger.note: Ecuador and Germany have met twice all-time: the 2006 World Cup group stage (Germany 3-0) and a 2013 friendly (Germany 4-2). No other meetings on record.

c-r3-sco-bra.source: Individual World Cup match results cross-checked via web search against Wikipedia group-stage pages and secondary head-to-head summaries: 1974 (0-0 draw), 1982 (Brazil 4-1, scorers Zico/Oscar/Eder/Falcao vs Narey), 1990 (Brazil 1-0, Muller), 1998 (Brazil 2-1, https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/1998_FIFA_World_Cup_Group_A, 10 Jun 1998).
c-r3-sco-bra.note: Scotland and Brazil had met at the World Cup on four prior occasions before this 2026 fixture (all in the group stage): 1974 draw, then three Brazil wins (1982, 1990, 1998). All-time (all-competitions) record — 10 meetings, Brazil 8 wins/2 draws per secondary sources — NOT included here: only 5 of the 10 individual scorelines could be independently confirmed, so exact goal aggregates for all-time remain unresolved. allTime scope intentionally left on the automated/capped value pending further research.

h-r3-uru-esp.source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/1950_FIFA_World_Cup_final_round (2-2, 9 Jul 1950); https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/1990_FIFA_World_Cup_Group_E (0-0, 17 Jun 1990); confirmed as the only two prior WC meetings via secondary head-to-head summary (Spain's 2026 win was their first-ever WC win over Uruguay).
h-r3-uru-esp.note: Uruguay and Spain had met at the World Cup twice before this 2026 fixture, both draws (1950, 1990). All-time (all-competitions) record — 10 total meetings per prose — NOT included here: only 3 of the 10 individual scorelines are confirmed, so all-time goal aggregates remain unresolved. allTime scope intentionally left on the automated/capped value pending further research.

sf-m2.source: Cross-checked via web search against the Argentina–England football rivalry Wikipedia article and multiple named sports outlets (ESPN, Yahoo Sports, FOX Sports); every individual date/score verified against at least one dedicated match-report source.
sf-m2.note: England and Argentina have met 5 times at the World Cup (1962, 1966, 1986, 1998, 2002) — confident this is the complete World Cup record. All-time: 11 matches verified (1951–2005), used as the allTime aggregate even though at least one source cites 15 total prior meetings — the 11 are real and fully sourced, the aggregate is a known floor rather than a fabricated total.

qf-m2.source: Cross-checked via web search against multiple named sports outlets (ESPN, Bolavip, Sports Mole, Yahoo Sports); each date/score independently confirmed.
qf-m2.note: Spain and Belgium have met twice at the World Cup (1986, 1990) — confident this is the complete World Cup record. All-time: 3 matches verified, used as a known floor even though a claimed 22 total meetings isn't fully resolved.

qf-m4.source: Cross-checked via web search against Yahoo Sports, Bolavip, and Wikipedia's Switzerland at the FIFA World Cup article; both dates/scores independently confirmed.
qf-m4.note: Argentina and Switzerland have met twice at the World Cup (1966, 2014) — confident this is the complete World Cup record, and both were Argentina wins. All-time: these are the only 2 matches independently verified; used as a known floor even though a claimed 7 total meetings isn't fully resolved.

r16-m1.source: Cross-checked via web search against ESPN, 11v11-cited sources, and dedicated match reports for each of the 5 matches; every date/score independently confirmed.
r16-m1.note: Paraguay and France have met exactly 5 times all-time (1958, 1998, 2008, 2014, 2017), matching every source's stated total — confident this is complete for both scopes. Two of the five were World Cup meetings (1958, 1998).

r16-m3.source: Cross-checked via web search against ESPN, Yahoo Sports, and Sofascore; every date/score independently confirmed. "Five times" total per sources includes the 2026 fixture itself.
r16-m3.note: Brazil and Norway have met exactly 4 times prior to this 2026 fixture (1988, 1997, 1998, 2006) — confident this is complete for both scopes; Brazil have never beaten Norway. One of the four was a World Cup meeting (1998).

r16-m4.source: Cross-checked via web search against englandfootball.com, Bleacher Report, and multiple named match-report sources; every date/score independently confirmed. Matches the "nine previous meetings" total cited by multiple sources exactly.
r16-m4.note: Mexico and England have met exactly 9 times all-time (1959–2010) — confident this is complete for both scopes. One of the nine was a World Cup meeting (1966).

r16-m5.source: Direct Wikipedia fetch (Portugal–Spain football rivalry article) for the qualifying/tournament matches, cross-checked against Sports Mole and 11v11-cited sources.
r16-m5.note: Portugal and Spain have met twice at the World Cup itself (2010, 2018) — confident this is the complete World Cup record. All-time: 10 matches verified (including 2 WC qualifying ties, which don't count toward the worldCup scope), used as a known floor even though 42 total meetings is claimed.

r16-m6.source: Cross-checked via web search against a chronological stats summary (X/Twitter @stats_feed) and individual ESPN/11v11-cited match reports for each date.
r16-m6.note: USA and Belgium have met exactly 6 times prior to this 2026 fixture (1930–2014) — confident this is complete for both scopes. Two of the six were World Cup meetings (1930, 2014).

r16-m8.source: Cross-checked via web search against ESPN, Sports Mole, and Wikipedia's 1994 FIFA World Cup Group A article — which corrected an initial source's misreported 1994 scoreline (Colombia won 2-0, not 1-0).
r16-m8.note: Switzerland and Colombia have met exactly 4 times all-time (1985, 1991, 1994, 2007) — confident this is complete for both scopes. One of the four was a World Cup meeting (1994).

r32-m2.source: Cross-checked via web search against ESPN and asuncintimes.com; both dates/scores independently confirmed. A source describing the 2002 match as "only the third senior meeting" implies an earlier, undated match this research could not verify — not included, per the verified-only rule.
r32-m2.note: Germany and Paraguay's only confirmed World Cup meeting is 2002. All-time: these 2 verified matches (2002 WC, 2013 friendly) are used as a known floor — a source implies at least one earlier meeting exists but its date/score couldn't be verified.

r32-m3.source: Cross-checked via web search against ESPN and Morocco Football Tribune. A 1999 friendly (Morocco won 2-1) is well-corroborated but no source gave an exact day-of-month, so it is excluded from matches[] per the verified-only rule — allTime reflects only the 2 fully-dated matches (2 of a real 3), a known floor.
r32-m3.note: Netherlands and Morocco's only World Cup meeting prior to this 2026 fixture is 1994 — confident this is complete for the World Cup scope.

r32-m4.source: Cross-checked via web search against ESPN, 365scores, national-football-teams.com, and FIFA.com's own report of the October 2025 friendly; every date/score independently confirmed. Matches the "15 meetings" total (14 prior + this 2026 fixture) cited by multiple sources exactly.
r32-m4.note: Brazil and Japan have met exactly 14 times prior to this 2026 fixture (1989–2025) — confident this is complete for both scopes. One of the fourteen was a World Cup meeting (2006).

r32-m5.source: Cross-checked via web search (ESPN-adjacent match reports). Only one of a claimed 8 all-time meetings could be independently dated/scored.
r32-m5.note: France and Sweden had never met at the World Cup before this 2026 fixture (confirmed via FIFA.com: "France and Sweden have always managed to avoid one another at the World Cup until very recently") — worldCup is genuinely zero, not a gap, but left un-overridden since the automated autoCapped flag can't be selectively cleared per-scope by this script (known limitation, not a fabrication risk). All-time uses the 1 verified match as a known floor against a claimed 8 total.

r32-m7.source: Cross-checked via web search against ESPN and Wikipedia's 2002 FIFA World Cup Group G article.
r32-m7.note: Mexico and Ecuador's only World Cup meeting prior to this 2026 fixture is 2002 — confident this is complete for the World Cup scope. All-time sources are contradictory (28 vs. 16 total meetings cited) and not resolvable with confidence; allTime here reflects just this 1 verified match as a known floor, not a claim of completeness.

r32-m11.source: Cross-checked via web search against UEFA.com and ESPN. A general summary's claim of a "June 2024" Croatia win could not be corroborated — the actual September and November 2024 Nations League matches found were a Portugal win and a draw respectively, so no Croatia win is included.
r32-m11.note: Only 3 of a claimed 10 all-time meetings could be independently verified — used as a known floor, not a claim of completeness. None were World Cup meetings (this 2026 fixture is Portugal and Croatia's first-ever World Cup meeting), so worldCup is not overridden.

r32-m12.source: Cross-checked via web search against Yahoo Sports (Spain v Austria history feature), footballdatabase.eu, and ESPN.
r32-m12.note: Spain and Austria's only World Cup meeting prior to this 2026 fixture is 1978 — confident this is complete for the World Cup scope (a widely-cited "most notable meeting between the two nations up until now"). All-time reflects these 3 verified matches as a known floor against a claimed 16 total.
